container-definition.description: Custom name of CloudWatch log group for a service associated with the container definition
container-definition.name: cloudwatch_log_group_name

container-definition.description: Number of days to retain log events. Set to `0` to keep logs indefinitely
container-definition.name: cloudwatch_log_group_retention_in_days

container-definition.description: When this parameter is true, networking is disabled within the container
container-definition.name: disableNetworking

container-definition.description: The entry point that is passed to the container
container-definition.name: entrypoint

container-definition.description: The FireLens configuration for the container. This is used to specify and configure a log router for container logs. For more information, see [Custom Log Routing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/using_firelens.html) in the Amazon Elastic Container Service Developer Guide
container-definition.name: firelensConfiguration

container-definition.description: The hostname to use for your container
container-definition.name: hostname

container-definition.description: The type and amount of a resource to assign to a container. The only supported resource is a GPU
container-definition.name: resourceRequirements

container-definition.description: Specifies whether Amazon ECS will resolve the container image tag provided in the container definition to an image digest
container-definition.name: versionConsistency

container-definition.description: Specified the log class of the log group. Possible values are: `STANDARD` or `INFREQUENT_ACCESS`
container-definition.name: cloudwatch_log_group_class

container-definition.description: If a KMS Key ARN is set, this key will be used to encrypt the corresponding log group. Please be sure that the KMS Key has an appropriate key policy (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/encrypt-log-data-kms.html)
container-definition.name: cloudwatch_log_group_kms_key_id

container-definition.description: The dependencies defined for container startup and shutdown. A container can contain multiple dependencies. When a dependency is defined for container startup, for container shutdown it is reversed. The condition can be one of START, COMPLETE, SUCCESS or HEALTHY
container-definition.name: dependsOn

container-definition.description: Linux-specific modifications that are applied to the container, such as Linux kernel capabilities. For more information see [KernelCapabilities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/APIReference/API_KernelCapabilities.html)
container-definition.name: linuxParameters

container-definition.description: When this parameter is true, the container is given read-only access to its root file system
container-definition.name: readonlyRootFilesystem

container-definition.description: The secrets to pass to the container. For more information, see [Specifying Sensitive Data](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/specifying-sensitive-data.html) in the Amazon Elastic Container Service Developer Guide
container-definition.name: secrets

container-definition.description: Time duration (in seconds) to wait before giving up on resolving dependencies for a container
container-definition.name: startTimeout

container-definition.description: Time duration (in seconds) to wait before the container is forcefully killed if it doesn't exit normally on its own
container-definition.name: stopTimeout

container-definition.description: A list of ulimits to set in the container. If a ulimit value is specified in a task definition, it overrides the default values set by Docker
container-definition.name: ulimits

container-definition.description: Specs for Credentials for gMSA (Windows containers).
container-definition.name: credentialSpecs

container-definition.description: A list of strings to provide custom labels for SELinux and AppArmor multi-level security systems. This field isn't valid for containers in tasks using the Fargate launch type
container-definition.name: dockerSecurityOptions

container-definition.description: A list of namespaced kernel parameters to set in the container
container-definition.name: systemControls

container-definition.description: The name of the service that the container definition is associated with. Used in CloudWatch log group default name (if one is not provided)
container-definition.name: service

container-definition.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
container-definition.name: tags

container-definition.description: Container DNS search domains. A list of DNS search domains that are presented to the container
container-definition.name: dnsSearchDomains

container-definition.description: The container health check command and associated configuration parameters for the container. See [HealthCheck](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/APIReference/API_HealthCheck.html)
container-definition.name: healthCheck
container-definition.type:
object({
    command     = optional(list(string), [])
    interval    = optional(number, 30)
    retries     = optional(number, 3)
    startPeriod = optional(number)
    timeout     = optional(number, 5)
  })

container-definition.description: The log configuration for the container. For more information see [LogConfiguration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/APIReference/API_LogConfiguration.html)
container-definition.name: logConfiguration

container-definition.description: When this parameter is true, a `TTY` is allocated
container-definition.name: pseudoTerminal

container-definition.description: The OS family for task
container-definition.name: operating_system_family

container-definition.description: The list of port mappings for the container. Port mappings allow containers to access ports on the host container instance to send or receive traffic. For task definitions that use the awsvpc network mode, only specify the containerPort. The hostPort can be left blank or it must be the same value as the containerPort
container-definition.name: portMappings
container-definition.type:
list(object({
    appProtocol        = optional(string)
    containerPort      = optional(number)
    containerPortRange = optional(string)
    hostPort           = optional(number)
    name               = optional(string)
    protocol           = optional(string)
  }))

container-definition.description: Container repository credentials; required when using a private repo.  This map currently supports a single key; "credentialsParameter", which should be the ARN of a Secrets Manager's secret holding the credentials
container-definition.name: repositoryCredentials

container-definition.description: Data volumes to mount from another container
container-definition.name: volumesFrom

container-definition.description: Determines whether CloudWatch logging is configured for this container definition. Set to `false` to use other logging drivers
container-definition.name: enable_cloudwatch_logging

container-definition.description: Determines whether the log group name should be used as a prefix
container-definition.name: cloudwatch_log_group_use_name_prefix

container-definition.description: The number of cpu units to reserve for the container. This is optional for tasks using Fargate launch type and the total amount of `cpu` of all containers in a task will need to be lower than the task-level cpu value
container-definition.name: cpu

container-definition.description: A key/value map of labels to add to the container
container-definition.name: dockerLabels

container-definition.description: The environment variables to pass to the container
container-definition.name: environment

container-definition.description: The links parameter allows containers to communicate with each other without the need for port mappings. This parameter is only supported if the network mode of a task definition is `bridge`
container-definition.name: links

container-definition.description: The soft limit (in MiB) of memory to reserve for the container. When system memory is under heavy contention, Docker attempts to keep the container memory to this soft limit. However, your container can consume more memory when it needs to, up to either the hard limit specified with the `memory` parameter (if applicable), or all of the available memory on the container instance
container-definition.name: memoryReservation

container-definition.description: The mount points for data volumes in your container
container-definition.name: mountPoints

container-definition.description: The name of a container. If you're linking multiple containers together in a task definition, the name of one container can be entered in the links of another container to connect the containers. Up to 255 letters (uppercase and lowercase), numbers, underscores, and hyphens are allowed
container-definition.name: name

container-definition.description: The user to run as inside the container. Can be any of these formats: user, user:group, uid, uid:gid, user:gid, uid:group. The default (null) will use the container's configured `USER` directive or root if not set
container-definition.name: user

container-definition.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
container-definition.name: region

container-definition.description: Container DNS servers. This is a list of strings specifying the IP addresses of the DNS servers
container-definition.name: dnsServers

container-definition.description: Specifies whether to enable Amazon ECS Exec for the tasks within the service
container-definition.name: enable_execute_command

container-definition.description: If the `essential` parameter of a container is marked as `true`, and that container fails or stops for any reason, all other containers that are part of the task are stopped
container-definition.name: essential

container-definition.description: A list of hostnames and IP address mappings to append to the `/etc/hosts` file on the container
container-definition.name: extraHosts

container-definition.description: When this parameter is `true`, you can deploy containerized applications that require `stdin` or a `tty` to be allocated
container-definition.name: interactive

container-definition.description: Container restart policy; helps overcome transient failures faster and maintain task availability
container-definition.name: restartPolicy

container-definition.description: Determines whether a log group is created by this module. If not, AWS will automatically create one if logging is enabled
container-definition.name: create_cloudwatch_log_group

container-definition.description: The command that's passed to the container
container-definition.name: command

container-definition.description: A list of files containing the environment variables to pass to a container
container-definition.name: environmentFiles

container-definition.description: The image used to start a container. This string is passed directly to the Docker daemon. By default, images in the Docker Hub registry are available. Other repositories are specified with either `repository-url/image:tag` or `repository-url/image@digest`
container-definition.name: image

container-definition.description: The amount (in MiB) of memory to present to the container. If your container attempts to exceed the memory specified here, the container is killed. The total amount of memory reserved for all containers within a task must be lower than the task `memory` value, if one is specified
container-definition.name: memory

container-definition.description: When this parameter is true, the container is given elevated privileges on the host container instance (similar to the root user)
container-definition.name: privileged

container-definition.description: The working directory to run commands inside the container
container-definition.name: workingDirectory

service.description: Determines whether the ECS tasks IAM role should be created
service.name: create_tasks_iam_role

service.description: A map of additional tags to add to the IAM role created
service.name: infrastructure_iam_role_tags

service.description: Configuration block for deployment controller configuration
service.name: deployment_controller

service.description: Enable to force a new task deployment of the service. This can be used to update tasks to use a newer Docker image with same image/tag combination, roll Fargate tasks onto a newer platform version, or immediately deploy `ordered_placement_strategy` and `placement_constraints` updates
service.name: force_new_deployment

service.description: Process namespace to use for the containers in the task. The valid values are `host` and `task`
service.name: pid_mode

service.description: ARN of the ECS cluster where the resources will be provisioned
service.name: cluster_arn

service.description: Determines whether the ECS service IAM role should be created
service.name: create_iam_role

service.description: Amount (in MiB) of memory used by the task. If the `requires_compatibilities` is `FARGATE` this field is required
service.name: memory

service.description: Existing IAM role ARN
service.name: task_exec_iam_role_arn

service.description: A map of IAM policy [statements](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/data-sources/iam_policy_document#statement) for custom permission usage
service.name: task_exec_iam_statements

service.description: Name of the service (up to 255 letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores)
service.name: name

service.description: Configuration block for rules that are taken into consideration during task placement (up to max of 10). This is set at the service, see `task_definition_placement_constraints` for setting at the task definition
service.name: placement_constraints

service.description: A map of IAM policy [statements](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/data-sources/iam_policy_document#statement) for custom permission usage
service.name: tasks_iam_role_statements

service.description: Description of the role
service.name: infrastructure_iam_role_description

service.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name`) is used as a prefix
service.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

service.description: ECS automatically redistributes tasks within a service across Availability Zones (AZs) to mitigate the risk of impaired application availability due to underlying infrastructure failures and task lifecycle activities. The valid values are `ENABLED` and `DISABLED`. Defaults to `DISABLED`
service.name: availability_zone_rebalancing

service.description: Specifies whether to enable Amazon ECS Exec for the tasks within the service
service.name: enable_execute_command

service.description: Existing IAM role ARN
service.name: iam_role_arn

service.description: Determines whether the IAM role name (`iam_role_name`) is used as a prefix
service.name: iam_role_use_name_prefix

service.description: Configuration block that specifies whether the scaling activities for the service are in a suspended state
service.name: autoscaling_suspended_state

service.description: Number of instances of the task definition to place and keep running
service.name: desired_count

service.description: List of security groups to associate with the task or service
service.name: security_group_ids

service.description: The ECS Service Connect configuration for this service to discover and connect to services, and be discovered by, and connected from, other services within a namespace
service.name: service_connect_configuration

service.description: Determines whether the IAM role name (`tasks_iam_role_name`) is used as a prefix
service.name: tasks_iam_role_use_name_prefix

service.description: Map of autoscaling policies to create for the service
service.name: autoscaling_policies

service.description: Assign a public IP address to the ENI (Fargate launch type only)
service.name: assign_public_ip

service.description: Map of arbitrary keys and values that, when changed, will trigger an in-place update (redeployment). Useful with `timestamp()`
service.name: triggers

service.description: If true, Terraform will wait for the service to reach a steady state before continuing. Default is `false`
service.name: wait_for_steady_state

service.description: Maximum session duration (in seconds) for ECS tasks role. Default is 3600.
service.name: tasks_iam_role_max_session_duration

service.description: Name to use on IAM role created
service.name: tasks_iam_role_name

service.description: Configuration block for the App Mesh proxy
service.name: proxy_configuration

service.description: Configuration block for volumes that containers in your task may use
service.name: volume

service.description: Configuration block for deployment settings
service.name: deployment_configuration

service.description: Enable to delete a service even if it wasn't scaled down to zero tasks. It's only necessary to use this if the service uses the `REPLICA` scheduling strategy
service.name: force_delete

service.description: List of subnets to associate with the task or service
service.name: subnet_ids

service.description: Existing task definition ARN. Required when `create_task_definition` is `false`
service.name: task_definition_arn

service.description: Existing IAM role ARN
service.name: infrastructure_iam_role_arn

service.description: Seconds to ignore failing load balancer health checks on newly instantiated tasks to prevent premature shutdown, up to 2147483647. Only valid for services configured to use load balancers
service.name: health_check_grace_period_seconds

service.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the service
service.name: timeouts

service.description: Docker networking mode to use for the containers in the task. Valid values are `none`, `bridge`, `awsvpc`, and `host`
service.name: network_mode

service.description: Security group ingress rules to add to the security group created
service.name: security_group_ingress_rules
service.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)

    from_port                    = optional(string)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")

    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(string)
  }))

service.description: The VPC ID where to deploy the task or service. If not provided, the VPC ID is derived from the subnets provided

service.name: service_tags

service.description: IAM role path
service.name: iam_role_path

service.description: Whether should track latest `ACTIVE` task definition on AWS or the one created with the resource stored in state. Useful in the event the task definition is modified outside of this resource
service.name: track_latest

service.description: Maximum number of tasks to run in your service
service.name: autoscaling_max_capacity

service.description: A map of additional tags to add to the security group created
service.name: security_group_tags

service.description: Information about the CloudWatch alarms
service.name: alarms

service.description: Wait timeout for task set to reach `STEADY_STATE`. Valid time units include `ns`, `us` (or µs), `ms`, `s`, `m`, and `h`. Default `10m`
service.name: wait_until_stable_timeout

service.description: Determines whether to enable autoscaling for the service
service.name: enable_autoscaling

service.description: Determines whether to create a task definition or use existing/provided
service.name: create_task_definition

service.description: Description of the role
service.name: tasks_iam_role_description

service.description: Whether terraform should wait until the task set has reached `STEADY_STATE`
service.name: wait_until_stable

service.description: Specifies whether to propagate the tags from the task definition or the service to the tasks. The valid values are `SERVICE` and `TASK_DEFINITION`
service.name: propagate_tags

service.name: iam_role_tags

service.description: Map of additioanl IAM role policy ARNs to attach to the IAM role
service.name: tasks_iam_role_policies

service.description: Map of autoscaling scheduled actions to create for the service
